message:
fix(agent): persist messages before error propagation to prevent data loss on failures

Previously, when an agent turn ended with an error, the `result?` early
return would skip `persist_new_messages()`, silently discarding any
assistant/tool messages generated before the failure. This caused
conversation history to be lost on transient API errors.

Additionally removes redundant error-message construction and event
dispatch (MessageStart/MessageEnd/TurnEnd) from the streaming error
path. The error was already being surfaced via AgentEnd, so the extra
events duplicated information and could confuse event consumers.

Changes:
- Call persist_new_messages() BEFORE the `?` operator so messages are
  saved even when the agent errors out
- Remove 26 lines of dead error-event construction that duplicated
  the AgentEnd event's error reporting

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
